validation.approved_by: orchestrator (nw-bugfix minimal roadmap; user approved minimal scope 2026-07-16)
validation.notes: Bugfix roadmap per /nw-bugfix: regression test (RED) + minimal fix (GREEN) collapse into one DES 3-phase cycle. Splitting RED and GREEN across two DES steps would leave step 01-01 permanently red, which can never reach COMMIT/PASS.

phases.name: Regression test and dirty-flag fix
phases.description: Reproduce ADO #5499 with a failing test, then apply the one-line dirty-flag fix in the work-tracking-system change handler.

steps.name: Auto-save fires on work tracking system switch
steps.scenario_name: auto-saves a work tracking system switch made on its own
criteria: Switching work tracking system with no other edit auto-saves the new workTrackingSystemConnectionId; test fails before fix
No auto-save fires on mount or after reloadAfterConflict
Unknown system name does not auto-save workTrackingSystemConnectionId 0
Team and portfolio component tests assert save called with the new connection id
pnpm test green and pnpm build zero errors zero warnings
steps.implementation_notes: ROOT CAUSE: handleWorkTrackingSystemChange (useModifySettings.ts:333-350) never sets hasInteractedRef.current = true, so the auto-save effect returns early at :268 and never schedules the debounce timer. FIX: add `hasInteractedRef.current = true;` as the first statement INSIDE the existing `if (system) {` block at :338 — NOT at the top of the function, because selectedConnectionId is `selectedWorkTrackingSystem?.id ?? 0` (:170) and flagging dirty on the null branch would auto-save connectionId 0 into an FK violation. Full RCA with file:line evidence: docs/feature/fix-work-tracking-system-switch/deliver/rca-context.md. STRICT SCOPE: this one line is the ONLY production change permitted.
